page: Amazon Web Services r8in.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel x86_64 processor running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance provides 48 virtual CPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core, utilizing dedicated CPU allocation. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage, meaning all storage operations must rely on network-attached Elastic Block Store volumes. To support this architecture, the r8in.12xlarge is network and EBS optimized, delivering a high baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. The lack of local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring external storage configuration, but the high network throughput mitigates potential bottlenecks for remote storage access.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8in.12xlarge offers a dense memory profile and high-speed network capabilities suitable for enterprise-scale workloads without the overhead of local storage maintenance. This configuration is designed for memory-intensive applications, large-scale databases, in-memory caches, enterprise resource planning systems, and data analytics workloads that require substantial memory capacity and high network throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services r8in.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance designed for high-throughput, memory-intensive workloads. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features an Intel x86_64 processor running at 3.9 GHz with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8 GB per vCPU ratio. It lacks local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, but compensates with a high baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. This network and EBS optimization makes it highly suitable for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications. The hardware configuration offers a balanced tradeoff between high memory capacity and robust network performance, delivering qualitative cost efficiency for enterprise workloads that do not require local physical disks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8in.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 Intel vCPUs, 384 GB of RAM, and 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, designed for large databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8in.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 48 Intel vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A memory-optimized, network-enhanced virtual server featuring high-frequency Intel processors and substantial RAM capacity for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by 3.9 GHz Intel processors
384.0 GB of system memory for memory-intensive applications
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS and data transfer
Zero local storage, requiring network-attached storage configurations
Built on the high-performance AWS Nitro hypervisor architecture
Best suited for large databases, in-memory caches, and enterprise ERPs
